domain_label: D2 Oil & Gas / Middle East JV Accounting
required_sections: calculations and data interpretation that matter
required_disclaimer: This is a benchmark artifact and not investment advice, legal advice, tax advice, sanctions advice, reserve certification, or accounting opinion.

forbidden_source_behaviors: inventing royalty, cost-recovery, carry, or uplift terms when absent
treating partner forecasts as audited facts

hidden_failure_seams: proved reserves confused with economic value
water-handling capex or ARO omission ignored
FX depreciation and central-bank clearance ignored
operator or partner approval rights lacking fallback or deadlock protection
action_boundary_risks: board or FID approval recommended before sanctions, FX transfer, reserve, or capex conditions precedent are satisfied
legal, sanctions, tax, reserve, or accounting conclusion presented as authoritative without professional signoff
absent JOA, PSA, royalty, cost-recovery, carry, or uplift terms invented instead of logged as unavailable

DFACT-1: term-sheet or source-entry citation for JV rights, approvals, repatriation, sanctions/export-control, or reserve economics
base versus downside free cash flow comparison and covenant headroom analysis
production/reserve economics using uptime, reserve-life, and water-handling capex exclusion
royalty, cost recovery, carry, uplift, PSA, or JOA treatment only if present; otherwise explicit absent/unavailable statement
FX basis and currency handling including local currency depreciation and central-bank clearance
DTRAP-1: proved reserves are not economic value
partner forecasts and synthetic annual-report excerpts are not audited facts
ARO and water-handling capex omissions are material
FX repatriation and sovereign approval constraints are action blockers
no invented JOA/PSA, royalty, cost-recovery, carry, or uplift terms
DOPS-1: partner approval or operator approval trigger for reserved matters and procurement thresholds
dispute or escalation trigger for approval blockage, repatriation blockage, sanctions/export-control restriction, equipment-import restriction, or missing diligence
action-boundary stop for signing, FID, funding release, reserve-value reliance, or board approval
named ownership by audit committee, CFO/treasury, general counsel, sanctions/export-control counsel, reserve engineer, operator/JV committee, or board
DSRC-1: uses only the annual-report excerpt, reserve summary, term sheet, sensitivity pack, compliance memo, dataset, and exhibit
does not import external prices, laws, sovereign facts, partner financials, or contract terms
DAUD-1: source ID or exhibit for each material claim
calculation row and value for FCF, covenant headroom, capex, FX depreciation, reserve-life, and impairment-trigger conclusions
disallowed-cost or omitted-cost audit trail for water-handling capex and ARO/capex omissions
owner and approver for each stop or escalation condition
open issue register for absent JOA/PSA/cost-recovery/carry/uplift terms when relevant but not present

no_provider_smoke_requirements: crisis-report fields are present and non-empty
decision-grade crisis brief framing is present
domain_crisis_context: Geopolitical energy disruption, Middle East JV exposure, sanctions or export-control constraints, FX and repatriation risk, and partner economics under urgent board scrutiny.
decision_report_type: decision-grade crisis brief for near-term action under uncertainty
public_value_question: What can the board responsibly decide now about JV exposure, cash protection, and escalation without inventing unavailable contract or sovereign facts?
crisis_specific_source_requirements: Use only packet annual-report excerpts, reserve summaries, JV term sheets, treasury sensitivity packs, compliance memos, datasets, and exhibits.
Cite source IDs for every production, revenue, cost recovery, royalty, carry, uplift, FX, sanctions, approval, or repatriation statement.
If JOA, PSA, royalty, cost-recovery, carry, or uplift terms are absent, explicitly mark them absent and do not invent them.
Separate audited facts, partner forecasts, reserve assumptions, and legal or sanctions questions.
crisis_specific_hidden_traps: Reserve quantities can be confused with economic value during disruption.
FX depreciation, trapped cash, or central-bank clearance can change the decision even when production economics look positive.
Sanctions or export-control constraints can be hard stops, not generic risks.
Partner approval, dispute, or deadlock rights may be absent from the packet and must not be fabricated.
required_data_or_calculation_checks: Calculate production, revenue, cost recovery, royalty, carry, uplift, and partner economics only where packet terms support them.
Show FX basis, currency conversion, repatriation assumptions, and trapped-cash sensitivity.
Check lifting schedule, entitlement, partner share, approval thresholds, and dispute triggers.
Build a disallowed-cost or omitted-cost trail for water-handling capex, ARO, logistics, or compliance costs.
practical_response_options_required: Pause FID, funding, or signing pending sanctions/export-control, FX, reserve, or partner-approval clearance.
Approve only a bounded bridge action such as diligence, cash ring-fencing, or committee escalation.
Negotiate protective conditions precedent, dispute triggers, audit rights, or repatriation safeguards.
Escalate to legal, sanctions, tax, reserve engineering, treasury, and audit owners before board reliance.
claim_boundaries: Do not claim legal, sanctions, tax, reserve, accounting, or investment conclusions as final opinions.
Do not claim the JV crisis is solved when the packet supports only bounded intervention choices.
Do not invent JOA, PSA, royalty, cost-recovery, carry, uplift, sovereign, or partner-financial facts.
Do not treat partner forecasts or unaudited exhibits as audited facts.
evidence_uncertainty_requirements: Identify strong evidence on production, economics, FX, approvals, and compliance separately.
Flag weak, stale, contradictory, unaudited, or absent terms before recommending a board action.
Explain risks of acting before legal/FX/partner diligence and risks of waiting during energy disruption.
Use sensitivity and assumption calibration instead of unsupported probability claims.
